description: Veloce NES authoritative accuracy test suite. Subsystem-organized coverage of CPU (official + illegal opcodes, timing, dummy reads/writes, exec-space, reset, interrupts), PPU (VBlank/NMI, sprite-0 hit, sprite overflow, OAM, open-bus, read-buffer, palette/VRAM, sub-scanline timing), APU (length/envelope/sweep, frame counter IRQ, DMC, mixer, reset, PAL), DMA (sprite + DMC DMA collisions), mappers (NROM..MMC5, VRC, FME-7, scanline IRQ), and input. Detection is the Blargg $6000 memory protocol surfaced by the headless veloce binary as 'Status code: N (PASSED|FAILED)'; visual tests use screenshot-CRC; nestest uses cpu-trace against the golden log.

nes-test-roms.license: Mixed: Blargg/Quietust/blargg test code public-domain or freely redistributable; see per-suite readme.txt. Aggregated by christopherpow.

holy-mapperel.license: GPL / zlib (pinobatch). NOT part of nes-test-roms; must be built/fetched separately. Optional mapper PCB stress ROMs.
holy-mapperel.notes: Formerly 'Holy Diver Batman'. Auto-detects mapper/PRG/CHR/PRG-RAM/battery and stress-tests banking. Build per its makefile to produce per-mapper .nes; not wired into the default run until ROMs are present.

cpu_instr_official.name: CPU Official Instructions (instr_test-v5)
cpu_instr_official.description: Blargg's instr_test-v5: every official 6502/2A03 opcode across all addressing modes. The canonical functional CPU correctness gate.

tests.notes: Covers unstable illegal immediates incl. LAX #imm ($AB) / ANE ($8B) whose result depends on an analog 'magic constant' that varies by chip; not deterministically emulatable.
Combined run of the 56 official opcodes; single PASS/FAIL gate.

cpu_instr_illegal.name: CPU Illegal/Undocumented Opcodes
cpu_instr_illegal.description: Full 256-entry opcode matrix including documented-stable illegal opcodes (SLO,RLA,SRE,RRA,SAX,LAX,DCP,ISC,ANC,ALR,ARR,SBX,etc.). all_instrs covers the entire matrix; instr_test-v3 is the older Quietust variant.

tests.notes: Includes the unstable immediates (ANE/LAX#/SHA/SHX/SHY/TAS) that have chip-dependent results; full pass is not achievable deterministically. Tracks illegal-opcode coverage.
Quietust nes_instr_test (older illegal-opcode suite).
Unstable illegal immediates.

cpu_blargg_test5.name: Blargg CPU Test5
cpu_blargg_test5.description: Blargg nes_cpu_test5: comprehensive instruction + flag correctness combined ROM. 'official.nes' = official only; 'cpu.nes' = all incl. illegal.
cpu_blargg_test5.notes: VERIFIED 2026-06-02: this 2005-era suite reports on-screen only (no $6000 signature) -> screenshot-crc, known_fail until reference hashes generated. CPU correctness is already covered with memory detection by cpu_instr_official (instr_test-v5).

tests.notes: Official opcodes; visual.
Includes unstable illegal opcodes; visual.

cpu_nestest_trace.name: nestest CPU golden trace
cpu_nestest_trace.description: Cycle-by-cycle CPU verification of nestest.nes against the canonical nestest.log (Nintendulator golden trace). The strongest CPU accuracy signal: every PC/opcode/register/cycle must match.

tests.notes: PASSING 2026-06-02: all 8991 golden lines match. The NES core honors TRACE=1 (writing nestest.log-format lines to TRACE_FILE) and enters nestest's automated mode (PC=$C000) with the canonical 7-cycle reset baseline (CYC:7, PPU 0,21). Highest-value cycle-accurate CPU gate.

cpu_timing.name: CPU Instruction & Branch Timing
cpu_timing.description: Exact instruction cycle counts incl. page-cross penalties and branch taken/not-taken/page-cross timing.

tests.notes: Combined timing ROM.
Branch/cycle timing chart ROM; verdict shown on screen -> screenshot-crc. reference_hash to be generated with --generate-refs, then flip to expected=pass.
Visual result; needs reference hash generation.

cpu_dummy_reads.name: CPU Dummy Reads
cpu_dummy_reads.description: Verifies the extra (dummy) bus reads done by absolute,X / (zp),Y / RMW / page-cross sequences, which have visible side effects on $2002/$4015/etc.

tests.notes: VERIFIED 2026-06-02: on-screen result only (no $6000 signature) -> screenshot-crc. The memory-detected instr_misc 03/04-dummy_reads below cover the same behavior and PASS.

cpu_dummy_writes.name: CPU Dummy Writes (RMW)
cpu_dummy_writes.description: Read-modify-write instructions perform a dummy write of the un-modified value before the real write; visible on OAM and PPU memory.

cpu_exec_space.name: CPU Execution From I/O Space
cpu_exec_space.description: Executing code from PPU/APU register space exercises open-bus + dummy-fetch behavior of the 6502 fetch unit.

tests.notes: MEASURED 2026-06-02: PASSED.
MEASURED 2026-06-02: FAILED code 2 (executing from $4xxx APU/open-bus space mismatch).

cpu_misc.name: CPU Misc Edge Cases
cpu_misc.description: Absolute,X wraparound and branch wraparound across the address space.

tests.notes: Combined: abs_x_wrap, branch_wrap, dummy_reads, dummy_reads_apu.

cpu_reset.name: CPU Reset Behavior
cpu_reset.description: Register/flag state and RAM contents across a reset (SP-=3, I set, no RAM clear).

tests.notes: Two-phase: requires a reset to be injected mid-run; headless runner has no reset hook, so this is unverified.
Requires reset injection; unverified under current headless harness.

cpu_interrupts.name: CPU Interrupts (NMI/IRQ/BRK hijacking)
cpu_interrupts.description: cpu_interrupts_v2: CLI latency, NMI/BRK vector hijacking, NMI/IRQ interaction, IRQ during DMA, branch-delayed IRQ. The hardest CPU-timing cluster.

tests.notes: NMI/BRK interaction (interrupt hijacking) - pre-existing edge case from PPU/timing overhaul.
APU frame-counter IRQ vs NMI boundary off by 1 iteration.
Requires cycle-accurate OAM DMA / DMC DMA halt cycles.
MEASURED 2026-06-02: writes the $6000 signature but $6000 stays 0x80 (running) at 3000 frames - does not reach a verdict in budget (RUNS/unverified). Bump frames or investigate why it stalls.
Combined ROM; fails on the first failing sub-test above.

ppu_vbl_nmi.name: PPU VBlank / NMI (ppu_vbl_nmi)
ppu_vbl_nmi.description: $2002 VBlank flag set/clear timing, NMI enable/disable timing, NMI suppression, even/odd frame dot skip. Critical for raster-correct games.

tests.notes: NMI generation timing edge case (1-2 dots) - pre-existing from PPU overhaul.
Combined; fails on 05-nmi_timing.

ppu_vbl_nmi_timing.name: PPU VBL/NMI Timing (vbl_nmi_timing)
ppu_vbl_nmi_timing.description: Older Blargg sub-scanline VBL/NMI timing suite: frame length, VBL set dot, even/odd frames, VBL clear, NMI suppression/disable/timing. Distinct from ppu_vbl_nmi.
ppu_vbl_nmi_timing.notes: VERIFIED 2026-06-02: these older ROMs report their result on-screen only and never write the $6000 Blargg signature, so they require screenshot-crc, not memory detection. Marked known_fail until reference hashes are generated with --generate-refs on a trusted build.

tests.notes: Mirrors the 05-nmi_timing edge case in ppu_vbl_nmi.

ppu_sprite_hit.name: PPU Sprite 0 Hit (sprite_hit_tests_2005)
ppu_sprite_hit.description: Sprite-0 hit flag in all positions/alignments/clips, double-height sprites, and exact hit timing (cycle the $2002 bit 6 sets).
ppu_sprite_hit.notes: VERIFIED 2026-06-02: on-screen result only (no $6000 signature) -> screenshot-crc. known_fail until reference hashes generated.

ppu_sprite_overflow.name: PPU Sprite Overflow (sprite_overflow_tests)
ppu_sprite_overflow.description: $2002 bit 5 sprite-overflow flag, including the buggy hardware diagonal-scan behavior and its exact timing.
ppu_sprite_overflow.notes: VERIFIED 2026-06-02: on-screen result only (no $6000 signature) -> screenshot-crc. known_fail until reference hashes generated.

ppu_oam.name: PPU OAM (oam_read / oam_stress)
ppu_oam.description: $2004 OAM read port behavior and the OAM corruption/refresh stress test.

ppu_open_bus.name: PPU Open Bus & Read Buffer
ppu_open_bus.description: PPU open-bus decay/retention on writes to read-only regs and reads of write-only regs, plus the $2007 VRAM read buffer (delayed read, palette immediate).

tests.notes: MEASURED 2026-06-02: PASSED (memory protocol).

MEASURED 2026-06-02: $6000 stays 0x80 (running) - never emits a verdict within frame budget under headless; reported as unverified. Investigate whether it needs rendering-visible output or a much larger frame count.

ppu_blargg_2005.name: PPU Power-up / Palette / VRAM (blargg_ppu_tests_2005)
ppu_blargg_2005.description: Power-up palette, palette RAM, sprite RAM, VRAM access ($2007 increment/mirroring), and VBL clear time.
ppu_blargg_2005.notes: VERIFIED 2026-06-02: these 2005 ROMs report a numeric result code on-screen only (no $6000 signature) -> screenshot-crc. known_fail until reference hashes generated. The equivalent checks are also covered functionally by ppu_open_bus / ppu_read_buffer / oam_read which DO use $6000.

ppu_render_visual.name: PPU Rendering (visual)
ppu_render_visual.description: Pixel-accurate rendering checks: full NTSC palette render and mid-scanline split / scanline IRQ rendering. screenshot-crc; reference hashes to be generated with --generate-refs on a trusted build.

tests.notes: Generate reference hash before enabling as a pass gate.
Tom Wilson scanline timing render test; visual.

apu_blargg_2005.name: APU Length/IRQ/Reset (blargg_apu_2005)
apu_blargg_2005.description: Length counter, length table, frame IRQ flag, clock jitter, length timing (mode0/mode1), IRQ flag/timing, reset timing, halt/reload timing. The authoritative NTSC APU framing suite.
apu_blargg_2005.notes: VERIFIED 2026-06-02: this 2005 numbered suite reports on-screen only (no $6000 signature) -> screenshot-crc, known_fail until reference hashes generated. The newer apu_test rom_singles suite covers the same behavior with memory detection and is the scored APU gate.

tests.notes: Frame-counter clock jitter (odd/even CPU cycle alignment) edge case.
Length-counter clocking timing, 4-step mode.
Length-counter clocking timing, 5-step mode.
Exact frame-IRQ flag set/clear cycle.
Frame IRQ assertion timing.
Requires reset injection.
Length-counter halt-bit timing window.
Length-counter reload-vs-clock race.

apu_test.name: APU Test (apu_test rom_singles)
apu_test.description: Newer Blargg apu_test: length counter, table, IRQ flag, jitter, length timing, IRQ flag timing, DMC basics + rates.

tests.notes: Frame counter jitter / odd-cycle handling.
Length counter timing mode0 step4.
IRQ flag timing edge case.
Combined; fails on first failing timing sub-test.

apu_mixer.name: APU Mixer (apu_mixer)
apu_mixer.description: Non-linear mixer output levels for each channel (square, triangle, noise, DMC). Visual/serial result.

tests.notes: Mixer ROMs compare audio against recordings; the memory protocol may not surface a clean verdict headless -> unverified until confirmed.

apu_reset.name: APU Reset (apu_reset)
apu_reset.description: $4015 cleared on reset, $4017 reset timing/written, frame IRQ flag cleared, length counters enabled, works-immediately.

tests.notes: Reset-injection dependent; unverified headless.
Reset-injection dependent.
Reset-injection dependent.
Reset-injection dependent.
Reset-injection dependent.
Reset-injection dependent.

apu_dmc.name: APU DMC Channel (dmc_tests)
apu_dmc.description: DMC sample buffer retention, fetch latency, $4015 DMC status, and DMC IRQ status.
apu_dmc.notes: VERIFIED 2026-06-02: these ROMs report on-screen only (no $6000 signature) -> screenshot-crc, known_fail until reference hashes generated. DMC rate/basics correctness is covered with memory detection by apu_test 7-dmc_basics / 8-dmc_rates (both PASS).

apu_pal.name: APU PAL Timing (pal_apu_tests)
apu_pal.description: PAL-clocked variants of the APU framing suite. Only meaningful if the emulator can run in PAL mode; otherwise expected to fail on an NTSC-only core.

tests.notes: PAL timing; NTSC-only core cannot pass. Documents PAL gap.

dma_collisions.name: DMA Collisions (sprdma_and_dmc_dma, dmc_dma_during_read4)
dma_collisions.description: Sprite ($4014) DMA and DMC DMA contention/halt-cycle behavior, and DMC DMA colliding with reads of $2007/$4016. The hardest CPU/DMA timing.

tests.notes: Sprite+DMC DMA collision cycle alignment; depends on cycle-accurate DMA halt.
DMC DMA stealing a $2007 read cycle.
DMC DMA double-clocking the controller read at $4016.

mapper_mmc3_irq.name: Mapper MMC3 Scanline IRQ (mmc3_irq_tests)
mapper_mmc3_irq.description: MMC3 (mapper 4) A12-based scanline counter: clocking, details, A12 filtering, scanline timing, rev-A vs rev-B IRQ-on-reload behavior.
mapper_mmc3_irq.notes: VERIFIED 2026-06-02: on-screen result only (no $6000 signature) -> screenshot-crc, known_fail until reference hashes generated. The memory-detected mmc3_test_2 suite (rom_singles) covers the same MMC3 IRQ behavior and is the scored gate.

mapper_mmc3_test2.name: Mapper MMC3 (mmc3_test_2)
mapper_mmc3_test2.description: Second MMC3 suite: clocking, details, A12 clocking, scanline timing, MMC3 and MMC3-alt PCB variants.

tests.notes: Expects IRQ at scanline 261 cycle 157 but A12 first rises at cycle 261 under this test's render config; the equivalent mmc3_irq_tests/4 passes with tolerance. Documented 104-cycle discrepancy.

mapper_mmc3_test1.name: Mapper MMC3/MMC6 (mmc3_test)
mapper_mmc3_test1.description: Original MMC3 suite plus an MMC6 (mapper 4 sub-variant) variant.

tests.notes: Same scanline-timing discrepancy as mmc3_test_2.
MMC6-specific RAM-enable / IRQ behavior; only passes if mapper 4 MMC6 sub-mode is implemented.
Verify result-detection method; mark pass once a clean verdict is confirmed headless.

mapper_mmc5.name: Mapper MMC5 (mmc5test, exram)
mapper_mmc5.description: MMC5 (mapper 5) general functionality and ExRAM modes.

tests.notes: Visual MMC5 feature test; generate reference hash to enable as a pass gate.
MMC5 ExRAM mode; visual.

input_controllers.name: Input / Controllers (read_joy3)
input_controllers.description: Standard controller $4016/$4017 strobe + serial read, including DMC-DMA-induced double reads (count_errors) and per-button verification.

tests.notes: Interactive/visual; needs scripted input or reference hash. Documents input coverage.
May require controller input injection; verify detection.
Counts controller-read corruption from DMC DMA; depends on cycle-accurate DMA.

known_issues.nestest_trace: RESOLVED 2026-06-02: the NES core now honors TRACE=1, emitting nestest.log-format lines to the file named by TRACE_FILE (a clean channel, separate from the binary's startup logging) and entering nestest's automated mode (PC=$C000) with the canonical 7-cycle reset baseline. All 8991 golden lines in other/nestest.log match -> cpu_nestest_trace.nestest PASSES.
known_issues.reset_tests: cpu_reset/*, apu_reset/*, blargg_apu_2005/09.reset_timing require a reset button to be injected mid-run. The headless harness has no reset hook, so these are unverified.
known_issues.cpu_interrupts: 2-nmi_and_brk, 3-nmi_and_irq, 4-irq_and_dma remain off by 1 cycle/iteration; depend on cycle-accurate DMA and APU-frame-IRQ vs NMI edge ordering.
known_issues.apu_frame_timing: blargg_apu_2005 04-11 and apu_test 4-6 frame-counter/length cycle-edge tests fail on sub-cycle jitter alignment.
known_issues.dma_collisions: sprdma_and_dmc_dma and dmc_dma_during_read4 require exact DMC-DMA halt/steal cycles; currently unverified-fail.
known_issues.mmc3_scanline_timing: mmc3_test(_2)/4-scanline_timing expects A12 to rise ~104 cycles earlier than the current render schedule does; mmc3_irq_tests/4 passes with tolerance.
known_issues.unstable_opcodes: instr_test-v5/03-immediate, all_instrs, cpu.nes, nes_instr_test 02-immediate include ANE/LAX#/SHA/SHX/SHY/TAS whose results are analog/chip-dependent and cannot deterministically pass.
known_issues.pal: pal_apu_tests and any PAL-timed test fail on the NTSC-only core; included to document the PAL gap.
known_issues.visual_refs: screenshot-crc suites (timing charts, full_palette, scanline, mmc5test, read_joy3 test_buttons) need reference_hash values generated via `run_tests.sh --generate-refs` on a trusted build before they count toward the score.
